text: 42.2g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for seeds, the fat profile is one of the biggest reasons they earn their place.
28.9g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for a seed, that is a huge win.
18.3g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein is a bonus, but not enough by itself.
magnesium at 94% daily value, zinc at 30% daily value, iron at 32% daily value stand out most. that gives this food real mineral support.

pros first: usually brings decent fat support. that is one of the category strengths. easy to sprinkle into meals. that practical versatility matters. easy to sprinkle into meals for a real upgrade. seeds often shine as support foods.

now the downsides: paper scores can overstate realistic serving sizes. people often eat far less than 100 grams. texture or prep can be a barrier. not everyone uses seeds consistently. real serving sizes are often small. some strong paper numbers are hard to cash out in practice.
A tier. Best as a support food that boosts meals instead of carrying them alone.
